title: TCP Connections, Accept Loops, Half-Closes, Deadlines, and Failure · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A protocol pairing room predicts byte, parser, state, ownership, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first bounded TCP connection lifecycle state machine for tcp connections, accept loops, half-closes, deadlines, and failure. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bounded TCP connection lifecycle state machine while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain ordered reliable byte-stream delivery while accounting for segmentation, retransmission, flow control, congestion, and missing message boundaries.
Assign listener, accepted connection, goroutine, buffer, deadline, and close ownership with bounded accept-error handling.
Set and refresh absolute read and write deadlines by protocol phase and classify timeout evidence without assuming cancellation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Tcp Reliable Stream Model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop protocol team handles case 1656 in a archival client parsing incomplete close-delimited responses. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify connection, deadline, completion, and close state without inventing message boundaries; preserve this operating constraint: the browser model cannot open a socket or claim packet-level proof; then saturate one stream window while another remains independently serviceable. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Tcp Reliable Stream Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain ordered reliable byte-stream delivery while accounting for segmentation, retransmission, flow control, congestion, and missing message boundaries. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Tcp Reliable Stream Model, keep Tcp Reliable Stream Model connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
